title: Today's AI Brief: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
summary: A look at the impact of agentic AI on organizational design and a Māori text-to-speech model.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
imageCaption: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
imageSourceTitle: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI

body.text: I'm checking out some fascinating developments in AI. It seems that as AI agents become more autonomous, or 'agentic,' companies are struggling to adapt their organizational designs. According to MIT Technology Review, 85% of organizations want to leverage agentic AI, but there's a growing disconnect between ambition and execution. This made me wonder, what's holding them back?

The article argues that traditional structures may not be well-suited for the emerging AI landscape. Companies need to rethink their design to ensure seamless integration and maximize the benefits of these technologies. It's a bit like trying to fit a square peg into a round hole – it's time to rethink the shape of the hole. I'm curious to see how companies will adapt to this new reality.
A Voice for the Māori Language

In other news, a new text-to-speech model is giving a voice to the Māori language, one of New Zealand's official languages. This development is significant not only for the Māori community but also for the broader discussion around AI and cultural sensitivity. By creating AI models that cater to specific languages and cultures, we can work towards a more inclusive and diverse AI landscape. It's amazing to see how AI can be used to preserve and promote languages.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
